title: Intensely Charred Jamaican Swede Hash
description: A quick-fix Jamaican dish featuring swede, mortar-pestle-pound, and macrobiotic-friendly ingredients, cooked in an agave-leaf vessel. This dish is perfect for a weeknight dinner.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
Using a mortar and pestle, pound the scotch bonnet pepper, onion, garlic, thyme, allspice, salt, and black pepper into a coarse paste.
In a large bowl, combine the swede cubes and the paste, tossing to coat.
Brush the agave-leaf vessel with olive oil and add the swede mixture.
Roast the swede mixture in the oven for 20 minutes, or until it is intensely charred.
Remove the vessel from the oven and let it cool for 5 minutes.
Serve the swede hash hot, garnished with fresh thyme.
Char the agave-leaf vessel over an open flame for 2 minutes, or until it is lightly toasted.
